title: Unstable Insertion Sort with Missing Swap

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, implement an unstable insertion sort algorithm to sort it in-place. The function should return true if the array is sorted after the function call, and false otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: The array of integers to be sorted.
statement.outputFormat: Return true if the array is sorted, false otherwise.
statement.notes: The algorithm should be unstable and not use any extra space. Time complexity should be as efficient as possible.

constraints.description: Array of integers to be sorted

hints.text: The algorithm should work for both already sorted and unsorted arrays.
The time complexity of the algorithm should be as efficient as possible.

editorial.title: Unstable Insertion Sort
editorial.intuition: The algorithm should work by iterating through the array and inserting each element into its correct position. If the current element is smaller than the previous elements, it should shift them to the right and insert the current element at the correct position.
editorial.bruteForce: A simple brute force approach would be to use two nested loops to compare each element with the previous elements and shift them if necessary.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would be to use a single loop that iterates through the array, comparing each element with its previous elements and shifting them if necessary.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution would be to use a single loop that iterates through the array, comparing each element with its previous elements and shifting them if necessary. However, the algorithm should be unstable and not use any extra space.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The algorithm is correct because it correctly shifts the elements to their correct positions, but it is unstable because it may swap equal elements.
editorial.dryRun: Let's try to sort the array [5, 4, 3, 2, 1] using the unstable insertion sort algorithm.
commonMistakes: Using extra space to store the sorted elements
Not correctly shifting the elements to their correct positions
